_doc:
Chapter 1 argument frames — the synthesis units the player commits to in the Crab dialogue (crab.json first_meeting_with_binder / after_binder_first_engagement options block). Each frame names the SHAPE of a legal argument: which evidence supports it, which statute it anchors to, whether it's well-fitted to the case, and what consequences selecting it carries (judicial_patience, halina_trust, burns_round_attempt).

Version 2 (2026-05-16) per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §3 decoy revision: replaces v1's funnelled single-answer design with four credible decoys plus one optional hard-wrong. The decoys are reachable, credible at the moment of choice, and consequential per §4. The previous auxiliary frames (third_party_non_cure, fair_hearing_article_6) are absorbed as Phase 2 sub-citations under defective_service_135bis rather than top-level frames. The previous wrong-shape frame (merits_defence) is renamed to substantive_defense and reshaped with real evidence support (payment receipts + lease chain).

The player's selected frame writes to chapter1.proposed_frame (string enum). The court-round controller reads proposed_frame at Phase 2 start and applies (judicial_patience_delta_on_select, halina_trust_delta_on_select, burns_round_attempt) per the field values below. Phase 2 then plays out the judge_reaction_template's lines and patience trajectory.

Two-pass authoring per AGENTS.md §Casebook authoring.
  Code owns: id, supporting_evidence, statute_anchor, source_npc_primary, strength_rating, well_fitted, court_round_unlock, requires_flags, judge_reaction_template, judicial_patience_delta_on_select, halina_trust_delta_on_select, burns_round_attempt.
  Design owns: display_name, summary, wrong_shape_correction, present_cue.

Schema:
  frames: { <frame_id>: {
    display_name (Design — short noun phrase shown in the options block),
    summary (Design — one-line description for the binder UI),
    supporting_evidence (Code — Array[String] of evidence_ids from evidence_ch1.json),
    statute_anchor (Code — string label; not a tag, just a reference for the present_cue),
    source_npc_primary (Code — which NPC primarily surfaces this frame's evidence: 'crab' / 'murrow' / 'whimsy' / 'halina' / 'none'),
    strength_rating (Code — 0..5 int; max support a fully-surfaced evidence chain can give this frame),
    well_fitted (Code — bool; the canonically correct frame for Chapter 1),
    court_round_unlock (Code — which casebook_judge_state value this frame unlocks Phase 2 citation for),
    requires_flags (Code — Array[String] of chapter1.* flags / predicates that must resolve true to propose this frame),
    judge_reaction_template (Code — string id into data/judge_reactions_ch1.json or equivalent; templates: 'approving_set_aside' / 'tolerant_try_again' / 'cool_dismissal' / 'sharper_really_your_theory' / 'icy_silence'),
    judicial_patience_delta_on_select (Code — int applied to chapter1.judicial_patience at frame-commit moment),
    halina_trust_delta_on_select (Code — int applied to chapter1.halina_trust at frame-commit moment),
    burns_round_attempt (Code — bool; true means committing this frame consumes one of three court-round attempts),
    wrong_shape_correction (Design — Crab's / Murrow's / Whimsy's quiet correction line when this frame is selected),
    present_cue (Design — Cula's first-spoken-line when the frame opens Phase 2),
    in_fiction_consequence (Design — additional narrative consequence beyond the meter deltas, e.g. NPC refusals)
  } }
_owner_code: id, supporting_evidence, statute_anchor, source_npc_primary, strength_rating, well_fitted, court_round_unlock, requires_flags, judge_reaction_template, judicial_patience_delta_on_select, halina_trust_delta_on_select, burns_round_attempt
_revision_note: Schema version 2 — decoy revision per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §3 (2026-05-16). proposed_frame enum value renames: merits_defence → substantive_defense; the previous third_party_non_cure and fair_hearing_article_6 are absorbed as Phase 2 sub-citations under defective_service_135bis (not top-level frames). The dialogue-editor enum validator reads chapter1.json registry; chapter1.json updated in this commit.

defective_service_135bis._status: Code-pass complete; Design text pending. The canonical well-fitted frame: defective service under KPC Article 135-bis § 2. Strength 5 (max) when fully surfaced — envelope + renewal + renumbering + optional bonus evidence. Phase 2 sub-citations available under this frame (formerly top-level frames): third_party_non_cure (resident at no. 7 cannot cure service — surfaces when Murrow's first_meeting fact about authority is read) and fair_hearing_article_6 (ECHR Article 6 supplementary — surfaces when has_rights_memo is true).

phase_2_sub_citations._doc: Absorbed from v1 top-level frame. The resident at no. 7 accepting the notice does NOT cure service under § 135-bis third clause. Available once Murrow's first_meeting has surfaced the authority-required fact.
Absorbed from v1 top-level frame. ECHR Article 6 procedural-fairness supplementary citation, available when the Rights Memo (Whimsy at Café Paragraf) is collected.

substantive_defense._status: Code-pass complete; Design text pending. Renamed and reshaped from v1's merits_defence — now a real decoy with payment-receipt evidence support. Wrong because Friday is too close to develop merits substance AND the procedural defect already wins the immediate hearing. Halina is silent on this frame (halina_trust_delta = 0) — she expects Cula to find the procedural angle but doesn't penalise the merits move; the substantive case is genuinely her position and Cula raising it isn't an insult.
substantive_defense.statute_anchor: Civil Code — lease obligations (out of place at the motion stage)

notice_period_failure._status: Code-pass complete; Design text pending. Notice-period defenses are the standard junior move when an eviction notice arrives mis-served; plausible because the dates DO look short. Wrong because the locational defect (wrong door) ends the matter before timing arithmetic gets to do work — the timing argument is colorable but procedurally subordinate. Halina mildly disappointed (-1) — the procedural angle (wrong door) is in plain sight on the documents she handed Cula; reaching for timing instead reads as missing the obvious.

standing_wrong_party._status: Code-pass complete; Design text pending. The 'sharp counsel testing a theory' frame. Aggressive, defensible, and the sort of argument a procedurally-literate junior would consider. Wrong because the lease assigned forward at the 2018 ownership transfer — the prior landlord remains a real party in interest under the assignment, and the bench will note this. Halina disappointed (-1) — she signed the lease with the prior owner and continues to deal with him; the standing theory implies she doesn't know who her landlord is, which is mildly insulting.
standing_wrong_party.statute_anchor: Civil Code — assignment of obligations on property transfer

incapacity_defense._status: Code-pass complete; Design text pending. The hard-wrong frame — the firm's moral compass made visible. Argues Mrs. Sikorska is elderly and therefore not competent to be served. Wrong because Polish civil procedure does not impute incapacity from age; the test is cognitive, not chronological. Game-punished: Halina audibly disapproves ('I am quite competent, Dr. Cula'), Crab refuses to assist the next round (recruited_crab path requires a post-court apology dialogue beat), judge icy silence. Halina_trust drops sharply (-4). The optional fifth frame per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §1; include unless QA balance breaks. Source_npc_primary is 'none' because no NPC surfaces this evidence as a frame pointer — it's Cula's own projection based on Halina's age being visible in the client meeting.
incapacity_defense.statute_anchor: Civil Code — capacity (chronological age does not impute incapacity)
incapacity_defense.in_fiction_consequence: Crab refuses to assist the next court round (post-court Cula→Halina apology dialogue beat required to restore Crab's participation); Halina visibly disapproves in court via a one-line interjection; the court reporter pauses typing.
